blocks.text: Perfect. Now let me run the DESeq2 analysis in R:

input.code:
# Load required packages
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Read counts and metadata
counts <- read.csv('counts_de.csv', row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
metadata <- read.csv('metadata_de.csv', row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)

# Ensure order matches
counts <- counts[, rownames(metadata)]

# Convert to integer matrix (required for DESeq2)
counts <- as.matrix(counts)
storage.mode(counts) <- "integer"

print(paste("Counts matrix:", nrow(counts), "genes ×", ncol(counts), "cells"))
print("Metadata:")
print(table(metadata$cell_type))

# Create DESeqDataSet
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts,
                              colData = metadata,
                              design = ~ cell_type)

print(paste("DESeqDataSet created:", nrow(dds), "genes ×", ncol(dds), "samples"))
